title: Murder
fullText: Whoever commits murder shall be punished with death, or imprisonment for life, and shall also be liable to fine.
plainLanguage: Intentional killing is a very serious offense.
exampleScenario: A person intentionally causes another person's death.

title: Culpable Homicide Not Amounting to Murder
fullText: Punishment for culpable homicide not amounting to murder.
plainLanguage: Killing without meeting full conditions of murder.
exampleScenario: Death caused in sudden fight without premeditation.
penalty: Up to life imprisonment or up to 10 years

title: Attempt to Murder
fullText: Whoever does any act with such intention or knowledge and under such circumstances that, if by that act he caused death, he would be guilty of murder, shall be punished.
plainLanguage: Trying to kill someone is punishable even if death does not occur.
exampleScenario: Stabbing someone with intent to kill, but victim survives.
penalty: Up to 10 years or life + fine

title: Rape
fullText: Punishment for rape.
plainLanguage: Sexual assault is punishable with severe imprisonment.

title: Assault or criminal force to woman with intent to outrage modesty
fullText: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any woman, intending to outrage or knowing it to be likely that he will outrage her modesty, shall be punished.
plainLanguage: Harassing or assaulting women is criminal.
exampleScenario: Unwanted physical contact with sexual intent.

title: Cheating and dishonestly inducing delivery of property
fullText: Whoever cheats and thereby dishonestly induces the person deceived to deliver any property shall be punished.
plainLanguage: Fraud and cheating for money/property is punishable.
exampleScenario: Taking money by fake promise and refusing to return.

title: Criminal Breach of Trust
fullText: Whoever, being in any manner entrusted with property, dishonestly misappropriates or converts it.
plainLanguage: Misusing entrusted property is a crime.
exampleScenario: Agent using entrusted funds for personal use.

title: Punishment for criminal breach of trust
fullText: Punishment for criminal breach of trust.
plainLanguage: Penalty for misusing entrusted property.
penalty: Up to 3 years, or fine, or both

title: Cheating
fullText: Whoever, by deceiving any person, fraudulently or dishonestly induces the person so deceived.
plainLanguage: Definition of cheating under law.
exampleScenario: Fake investment plan to deceive people.

title: Theft
fullText: Punishment for theft.
plainLanguage: Stealing movable property is punishable.
penalty: Up to 3 years, or fine, or both

title: Theft in dwelling house
fullText: Theft in any building, tent or vessel used as a human dwelling.
plainLanguage: Theft inside house/building is punished more seriously.

title: Robbery
fullText: Punishment for robbery.
plainLanguage: Robbery means theft with violence or threat.
penalty: Rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years + fine

title: Dacoity
fullText: When five or more persons conjointly commit or attempt to commit robbery.
plainLanguage: Group robbery by five or more people.
exampleScenario: Armed gang robbery by six persons.
penalty: Life imprisonment or rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years + fine

title: Criminal Trespass
fullText: Whoever enters into or upon property in possession of another with intent to commit an offence.
plainLanguage: Entering another's property unlawfully with wrongful intent.
exampleScenario: Entering rented land to intimidate tenant.

title: Punishment for criminal trespass
fullText: Punishment for criminal trespass.
plainLanguage: Penalty for unlawful entry.
penalty: Up to 3 months or fine or both

title: Cruelty by husband or relatives of husband
fullText: Husband or relative subjecting a woman to cruelty shall be punished.
plainLanguage: Domestic cruelty against wife is punishable.

title: Dowry Death
fullText: Where the death of a woman is caused by burns or bodily injury within seven years of her marriage and shown to be related to dowry demand.
plainLanguage: Deaths linked to dowry harassment are severely punished.
exampleScenario: Bride dies after persistent dowry demands.

title: Voluntarily causing hurt
fullText: Punishment for voluntarily causing hurt.
plainLanguage: Causing physical pain/injury without grave circumstances.
penalty: Up to 1 year or fine or both

title: Voluntarily causing hurt by dangerous weapons or means
fullText: Whoever causes hurt by dangerous weapons or means.
plainLanguage: Injury using weapons is punished more seriously.
penalty: Imprisonment up to 3 years, or fine, or both

title: Criminal Intimidation
fullText: Punishment for criminal intimidation.
plainLanguage: Threatening someone with harm is punishable.
penalty: Up to 2 years or more for aggravated threat

title: Word, gesture or act intended to insult the modesty of a woman
fullText: Word, gesture or act intended to insult the modesty of a woman.
plainLanguage: Verbal/gestural harassment of women is punishable.

title: Criminal Conspiracy
fullText: Punishment of criminal conspiracy.
plainLanguage: Planning a crime with others is punishable.

title: Acts done by several persons in furtherance of common intention
fullText: Acts done by several persons in furtherance of common intention.
plainLanguage: Shared criminal intention creates shared liability.

title: Manner in which complaint shall be made
fullText: A complaint may be filed by a consumer or recognised association before the appropriate commission.
plainLanguage: How a consumer complaint is filed.
exampleScenario: Customer files complaint for defective appliance.

title: Request for obtaining information
fullText: A person who desires to obtain information shall make a request in writing or through electronic means.
plainLanguage: How to submit RTI request.

title: Computer related offences
fullText: If any person dishonestly or fraudulently does any act referred to in section 43.
plainLanguage: Unauthorized computer access and damage are punishable.
penalty: Imprisonment up to 3 years or fine
